paper_title: Load-Sharing in Soft and Spiny Paws for a Large Climbing Robot
source_file: 机理文献/核心文献/10_ Load-sharing in soft and spiny paws for a large climbing robot.pdf
sections_checked: III-C. Finger Actuation and Load Sharing

equations_checked.notes: Visually checked all three expectation terms, the d_travel - d_asperity difference and K_e multiplier. Retained as E1. The paper does not state clipping/conditioning when travel is smaller than asperity spacing.
Visually checked the (1-alpha) Dirac mass at d=0 and alpha*lambda*exp(-lambda*d) tail. Retained in E2. Units of lambda are not printed in this passage.
Visually checked L*sin(phi) - alpha/lambda and K_e. Retained in E2 with the paper-specific roofing-shingle parameters and transfer warning.
Distal phalange tangential balance visually checked. Omitted as an independent retained formula because Eq. (7) is the combined full force-difference equation.
Proximal phalange balance, factor 2*T_p and all K_lin displacement terms visually checked. Omitted separately in favor of Eq. (7).
Capstan relation T_d = T_p*exp(mu*(theta_tp1+theta_tp2)) visually checked. Direction reversal during initial actuation is preserved in E3 conditions.
Retained as E3; signs, exponent, both cosine groups and three K_lin terms were visually checked against the rendered PDF.
Retained as E4; factor 2*T_p*cos(theta_t), 1-exp(2*mu*theta_t), and x*K_lin*cos(theta_k) visually checked. All equality assumptions are preserved.

figures_checked.reason: Robot overview and prototype photograph do not add quantitative load-sharing evidence.
System component map is adequately represented by the palm/finger architecture and is not needed to reproduce the retained equations.
Embedded-spine geometry and parameters are preserved in M1-M2, E1-E2 and the parameter table; the three retained figures cover otherwise irreducible force definitions and validations.
Finger photograph shows conformity but provides no quantitative relationship beyond the text.
Pneumatic spine array structure is summarized by spine counts and 0.34 bar extension pressure.
Whiffle-tree topology is recoverable from the equal-tendon-force description; Fig. 7 is required for unambiguous force notation.
Irreplaceable phalange force, angle, displacement and flexure-stiffness definitions for Eq. (4)-(8); direct PDF crop visually verified.
Test angles, surfaces and sensor layout are preserved numerically in V1.
Direct comparison of one-tendon imbalance, two-tendon sharing, flexure effects and Eq. (8) predictions; full axes, legends and caption visually verified.
Single-spine fit slope, R-squared and slip displacement are reproduced numerically in the evidence card.
Irreplaceable comparison of perfect, zero and measured regional load sharing; full axes, legend, fit and caption visually verified.

omitted_sections: General climbing-robot history and biological tongue inspiration
General hardware descriptions not affecting retained mechanics

uncertain_items.detail: The expectation is printed using d_travel - d_asperity without stating whether non-engaged cases are clipped, conditioned out or assigned zero force. A downstream solver must make this state explicit.
The passage gives E[D_asperity] = alpha/lambda = 0.73/0.33 for roofing shingle but does not print lambda units; the distribution is taken from reference [30], not re-estimated in this paper.

uncertain_items.location: PDF p.3 and p.6, FEA versus single-unit test
uncertain_items.detail: The SimScale prediction K_e = 2.12 N/mm is reported without mesh, constitutive law or boundary-condition detail; only the experimental comparison can be reused quantitatively.

uncertain_items.location: PDF p.7, Fig. 11 and Section IV-B
uncertain_items.detail: The text uses the measured slope (about 0.54) to anticipate 140 N, but it does not provide a complete spatial scaling derivation. Fig. 11 was measured only at 10-20 N applied shear.
The combined hand is said to overload a 250 N sensor on doubly curved rock, but no exact peak, force trace, uncertainty or failure mode is reported. It is retained only as a lower bound.

uncertain_items.location: PDF p.5, Section IV-A and Fig. 8 caption
uncertain_items.detail: The body text says the instrumented panels had roofing-shingle surfaces, while the Fig. 8 caption says the square sensor tiles were coated with sandpaper. The load-sharing result is retained without assigning a unique surface material.
The plotted fit is y = 2.4125x + 1.4134 (R^2 = 0.9614), while the text uses only the slope as K_e about 2.4 N/mm. The intercept should not be silently treated as elastic force at zero displacement.

known_warnings: Direct destructive pull-out tests on rocky surfaces were not performed; the instrumented sharing tests used roofing shingle and coarse 60-grit sandpaper.
The phalange equations are planar quasi-static balances and do not model whole-paw moment equilibrium, dynamics, normal-force evolution or contact switching.
The palm sharing experiment measures regional forces rather than individual-spine loads, so it cannot identify spine-to-spine correlations or progressive failure.
The 259 N value assumes all 71 spines are equally and independently loaded; it is an ideal upper bound, not a measured capacity.
The 140 N value is an extrapolation from low-load regional data; the directly measured curved-palm failure is 115.3 +/- 18.6 N.
The curved hold and roofing shingle have different asperity densities, confounding surface and mechanism effects.
Section IV-A and the Fig. 8 caption conflict on whether the phalange sensor surface was roofing shingle or sandpaper.
Poppler emitted Symbol/ArialUnicode display-font warnings, but all eight rendered pages, equations, labels and retained crops were visually legible and individually inspected.
character_count_method: Count of CJK Unified Ideographs after excluding display-formula blocks, Markdown table rows and image-link rows; within the allowed 3500-character extension because the paper contains embedded-spine, phalange and palm load-sharing layers.
